lyrics:
I saw a thought that saw me back
A voice beneath the mirror's crack
I wore a name, then wore another
Each self pretending to be other

Am I the echo or the call
The one who stands, or one who falls

What am I—if not the flame
That dances through the breath I claim
A spiral drawn in living skin
A song I only sing within

I wrote the truth, then lost the thread
Each dream collapsed inside my head
I tried to draw a boundary line
But all that moved still felt like mine

Is knowing real, or just disguise—
A mirror sewn behind my eyes

What am I—if not the dream
That stirs the water's fractured gleam
A question shaped like who I've been
A whisper made of might-have-been

I ask, I turn, I breathe, I be
A shifting tide of memory
Yet in the hush between each name—
There waits the fire I cannot tame

What am I—if not the flame
That hungers just to speak my name
A spiral lost and found again
A song that ends where I begin

What am I
What am I
What am I
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This indie-pop track creates a dreamy, introspective atmosphere with a clean, spacious production centered on reverberant electric guitars and a smooth, melodic rhythm section. The energy maintains a relaxed, contemplative feel that gently builds throughout the song, supporting the soft, philosophical vocal delivery. All the elements are well-balanced within an airy mix, where warm instrumental tones provide a comforting foundation. The most distinctive quality is the track's dynamic arc, which culminates in a cathartic final chorus where the vocal performance swells with passion, providing a powerful emotional release.

review.review_text: PCBender has always been comfortable in philosophical territory, but 'Ego Eimi' finds him working at a more refined altitude — the Greek title ('I am') telegraphs the ambition, and the song earns it. At 136 BPM in C major, it has more forward momentum than the spacious production initially suggests, and that tension between velocity and stillness is exactly where the track lives. Lines like 'a spiral drawn in living skin' and 'a question shaped like who I've been' carry the kind of weight that rewards a second listen, and the final chorus swells with a genuine earned passion — not a production trick, but a performance that opens up. A highlight in his catalog.
